quotes.quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an implemented idea-filtering channel for novelty using an LLM connected to literature and web tools, with discard decisions controlling which ideas proceed.

quotes.quote: After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an implemented channel where experiment results are interpreted textually and routed into replanning and subsequent experiment implementation.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
quotes.rationale: Documents an LLM reviewer and a property-specific comparison of its generated paper decisions to external OpenReview ground truth decisions.

quotes.quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
quotes.rationale: States a functional limitation of the automated reviewer relative to standard review practice.

quotes.quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
quotes.rationale: States an input-modality limitation affecting the reviewer and broader system assessment of papers with figures.

quotes.quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.
quotes.rationale: Reports an agreement-style reviewer variant result: ensembling did not improve performance but may reduce variance.

quotes.quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an implemented memory-admission path where completed ideas and reviewer feedback are added to the archive for repeated operation.

diagnostics.consequence: The novelty gate may affect which ideas are discarded without reported channel-specific validation of its error rate or bias.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports an implemented LLM-and-tool novelty filter but does not report reliability evidence for that exact novelty-filtering channel.

diagnostics.consequence: Reviewer judgments may miss issues that require author interaction or figure inspection, limiting confidence in terminal paper assessment.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

diagnostics.statement: The automated reviewer is reported as unable to ask rebuttal questions and unable to view figures.

diagnostics.consequence: Generated papers should not be treated as final scientific claims without practitioner follow-up.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

diagnostics.statement: The packet explicitly warns against taking the scientific content of this version at face value.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

recommendations.proposed_action: Treat generated papers as leads for practitioner follow-up rather than accepted scientific conclusions.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: All generated papers are labeled as follow-up leads, not final validated findings.
success_check.evidence_needed: Workflow records or publication artifacts showing the stated status of generated papers.
success_check.observable: Generated-paper outputs carry a non-final status requiring practitioner follow-up.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add rebuttal-question handling and figure-viewing capability before relying on automated reviewer judgments for paper assessment.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reviewer runs demonstrate both author-question handling and direct figure inspection.
success_check.evidence_needed: Reviewer execution traces including rebuttal questions and vision-based figure inputs.
success_check.observable: Automated reviewer can ask author questions and evaluate figures directly.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

recommendations.proposed_action: Evaluate the novelty filter against a labeled prior-art similarity set before using discard decisions as a gate.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported false discard and false retain rates on the labeled similarity set.
success_check.evidence_needed: A channel-specific evaluation dataset and measured novelty-filter outcomes.
success_check.observable: Novelty-filter discard decisions are compared with labeled prior-art similarity judgments.

memory_structure: archive of scientific findings containing completed ideas and reviewer feedback
